title: Comforting Charred British Lamb Sunday Roast
description: A traditional British lamb roast with a charred crust, featuring papelon and a sweet glaze, cooked to perfection in a tagine pot. This dish is low in glycemic index, making it a great option for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 220 degrees Celsius.
In a small bowl, mix together papelon, olive oil, garlic, honey, apple cider vinegar, thyme, salt, and black pepper to make the glaze.
Season the lamb shoulder with salt and black pepper.
Place the lamb shoulder in the tagine pot and brush the glaze all over the lamb.
Roast the lamb in the preheated oven for 20 minutes to char the crust.
Reduce the oven temperature to 180 degrees Celsius and continue roasting for 2 hours, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 70 degrees Celsius.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest for 15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Strain the juices from the tagine pot and serve alongside the lamb.
